notes: Fixture registry. The pass fixture is the canonical state-oriented shape applied to a single domain (story-graph-ops): '## Story Model / ## Documents / ## Diagrams / ## Code' as markdown headings; every top-level describe is a domain concept ('a Story Map', 'a Markdown document', 'a diagram Story Map', 'a code Story Map', 'a DrawIO Story Map', 'a TypeScript story-spec Story Map'); Diagrams and Code apply the abstract-subject-then-concrete-backends pattern; every leaf is 'it should ...' observing an outside-verifiable result. Fail fixtures each isolate one violation so a scanner (once written) can target it directly.

cases.description: Canonical passing shape for a domain with multiple format backends. Categories are `##` headings; every top-level describe is a domain-concept subject; leaves observe results, not mechanics; state elaborations use `with` and `that`; Diagrams and Code apply the abstract-subject-then-concrete-backends pattern with `a diagram Story Map` / `a code Story Map` as the abstract subjects and `a DrawIO Story Map` / `a TypeScript story-spec Story Map` as thin concrete backends. Every rule reports clean.

Primary target: state-oriented-hierarchy. Describes are bare class names (StoryMap, Epic, SubEpic); leaves restate the operation as if the class were the actor ('should add an Epic to a StoryMap'). No subject noun phrase, no state buildup, no observations of state. Also fires category-headings-not-subjects (top-level 'Story Model' is a category label, not a subject); state-oriented-hierarchy independently flags 'Story Model' as not a valid subject noun phrase. Note: 'StoryMap', 'Epic', 'SubEpic' pass subjects-are-domain-concepts because they are legitimate domain concepts (the rule targets plumbing subjects like '-Node', '-Ast', '-Synchronizer', not domain concepts that happen to be written in CamelCase).

gap.action: New rule + scanner needed. See rules/state-oriented-hierarchy.md. Until a scanner is written, this fixture serves as a permanent regression example.

cases.description: Primary target: observations-are-results-not-mechanics. Every leaf observes an internal mechanic — a super call ('via super.updateSelf'), a factory name ('createChildXxx'), a private helper ('findMatch'), a call-order fact ('first via ...', 'before creating any new child'), or an absence-of-event ('should never be replaced'). Also fires: subjects-are-domain-concepts (subjects 'a StoryNode', 'a DiagramStoryNode', 'a CodeStoryNode' are code stereotypes with no independent domain meaning); domain-practice-alignment (same subjects aren't in the domain glossary); business-readable-language (leaves are saturated with engineering vocabulary like translateFrom, super.updateSelf, findMatch, childCollections). All four rules point at the same underlying failure from different angles.

gap.action: New rule + scanner needed. See rules/observations-are-results-not-mechanics.md. Until a scanner is written, this fixture serves as a permanent regression example.

cases.description: Primary target: subjects-are-domain-concepts. Every top-level describe is a code-level class name, not a domain concept: 'a LanguageAst' and 'a TypeScriptAst' are compiler-implementation stereotypes; 'a CodeStoryNode' and 'a DiagramStoryNode' are abstract class names exposed instead of the domain concepts they represent ('a code Story Map', 'a diagram Story Map'); 'a RowPositions' is a layout helper; 'a MarkdownSynchronizer' and 'a TypeScriptSynchronizer' are service-class stereotypes. Also fires domain-practice-alignment for the same reason (code stereotypes aren't in the domain glossary). State-oriented and observations-as-results pass because those aspects were kept well-formed; the failure is in the choice of subject.

gap.action: New rule + scanner needed. See rules/subjects-are-domain-concepts.md. Until a scanner is written, this fixture serves as a permanent regression example.

cases.description: Diagrams and Code sections skip the abstract domain subject and jump straight to concrete backends. Every backend then duplicates the same structural, reconciliation, and folder-tree observations verbatim under its own subject. State-oriented, subject-legitimate, and observation-as-result rules all pass — but adding a new backend requires copy-pasting every shared observation, and the specifications will silently drift over time.

gap.action: New rule + scanner needed. See rules/abstract-subject-then-concrete-backends.md. Until a scanner is written, this fixture serves as a permanent regression example.

cases.description: Primary target: category-headings-not-subjects. Organizational category labels ('Story Model', 'Documents', 'Diagrams', 'Code') are used as top-level describes rather than as `##` markdown headings. Also fires: state-oriented-hierarchy (the labels are bare CamelCase / bare noun-groups, not valid subject noun phrases per the updated rule) and domain-practice-alignment (organizational scaffolding isn't in the domain glossary). All three rules point at the same underlying shape failure — the top-level shouldn't have described these at all.

gap.action: New rule + scanner needed. See rules/category-headings-not-subjects.md. Until a scanner is written, this fixture serves as a permanent regression example.
